page: OVHcloud b3-640 is a general-purpose cloud server powered by AMD EPYC Milan processors using the x86_64 architecture. It features 160 dedicated physical cores and 160 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 640 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided by a 400 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The server achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as Passmark CPU and memory evaluations. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is highly efficient, ranking in the top 10%, while large block read and write operations maintain a strong top 25% standing. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation workloads also register in the top tier, demonstrating high parallel processing efficiency.

While the server excels in raw compute and prompt processing for large language models, text generation speeds for larger models fall into the average to poor categories due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. The server is highly cost-efficient for high-density, CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive local storage. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, Redis databases, static web serving, and parallel data processing tasks.
description: OVHcloud b3-640 is a high-density general-purpose server featuring 160 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 640 GB of RAM and a 400 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, this server delivers top-tier CPU and memory performance, placing in the top 10% for multi-core stress-ng benchmarks and Passmark CPU tests. It features a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX and AES. While highly efficient for parallel processing, software compilation, and Redis deployments, its lack of GPU acceleration limits its efficiency for large-scale LLM text generation. It represents a robust, resource-dense option for demanding CPU-bound workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-640 is a general-purpose server featuring 160 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 640 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for demanding parallel workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-640 features 160 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 640 GB RAM, and 20 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core compute performance.
tagline: A high-density, dedicated AMD EPYC server offering 160 physical cores and balanced memory for demanding parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: 160 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyperthreading
640 GB RAM providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
400 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for software compilation, Redis, and parallel processing
